refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Robust network link monitoring and error correction
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundant channels can be implemented within existing hardware constraints.
Risk of increased complexity leading to maintenance challenges.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating error correction mechanisms in future iterations to further improve availability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication management and link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but link availability logic is not centralized here.
Vehicle module depends on link but does not manage availability.
Primary module responsible for link availability management.
Monitors safety alerts but not directly responsible for link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with other modules requiring link status.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate redundancy within Teleoperation module to mitigate single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mapping and minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if link availability logic spreads outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link monitoring is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding error correction DP in next iteration.
Assess redundancy within Teleoperation IM to reduce single point of failure risk.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding error correction and redundancy considerations will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

revise.why: To incorporate additional design parameters such as error correction and adaptive bandwidth management to improve link availability robustness.

assumptions: Redundant communication channels can be implemented within hardware constraints.
Link availability is critical and safety-related.
open_risks: Single point of failure in Teleoperation module.
Complexity of managing redundancy and failover.
evidence_needed: Performance data on redundant channel failover effectiveness.
Verification results of link availability metrics.
